description_long: This rectangular manhole cover is set within a concrete frame and features a weathered, aged appearance. The cover itself is partially overgrown with moss and small patches of grass, particularly on one side, while the other side is covered with gravel and dirt. The metal frame is rusted and corroded, with visible bolts at the corners, and the surface is uneven due to the accumulation of organic debris and broken pavement. The surrounding pavement is a mix of concrete and embedded stones, with some leaf litter and signs of erosion along the edges. The overall look suggests it has been exposed to the elements for a long time, blending into an urban or suburban sidewalk or street environment.
description: A rectangular, weathered manhole cover set in concrete, partially covered with moss, gravel, and debris, showing signs of rust and age.
description_view_0: The cover is seen from a wide angle, highlighting the mossy, green patches on the left and the gravelly, eroded area on the right.
description_view_1: This view emphasizes the depth of the moss and the unevenness of the surface, with the concrete frame and bolts more visible.
description_view_2: The right side shows more gravel and less moss, with leaf litter and debris along the bottom edge.
description_view_3: The left edge is more eroded, with a clearer view of the transition between moss and gravel, and the concrete frame is prominent.
attribute_pattern_design: Irregular, with patches of moss, gravel, and some embedded stones; no geometric or symbolic motifs visible.
